item.title: Buffett Remains Open to Making Donations to Gates Foundation
item.description: Warren Buffett said he’s still open to making donations to the Gates Foundation even after the government’s release of the Jeffrey Epstein files reignited scrutiny of Bill Gates’ ties to the late financier and sex criminal.

item.title: William Hill Closes 200 Betting Shops, Blames UK Tax Increases
item.description: The owner of William Hill plans to shutter roughly 200 betting shops in the UK, putting a significant number of jobs at risk, following a crackdown on the gambling sector by Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves.

item.title: Activist Investor Irenic Is Said to Build Stake in Snap
item.description: Activist investor Irenic Capital Management has built a position in social media company Snap Inc. and is pushing for changes to improve financial and operating performance, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Trump Calls on Allies to Seize Hormuz as War Frustration Mounts
item.description: US President Donald Trump called on other nations to help wrest control of the Strait of Hormuz as Iran maintained missile fire across the Persian Gulf, with one strike hitting a fully laden oil tanker.

item.title: Volvo’s Owner Wants Closer Ties With Chinese Parent to Ease Pain
item.description: Volvo Car AB should start producing its Chinese sister brand’s models at its factories as the auto industry grapples with overcapacity, according to its billionaire owner Li Shufu.

item.title: Samsung Adds Blood Pressure Tracking to the Galaxy Watch
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. is the latest company to introduce a blood pressure monitoring feature for wearable devices, underscoring how fitness gadgets are increasingly blurring the lines with medical equipment.

item.title: Iran’s Crackdown on Starlink Sellers Hits Rare Link to Internet
item.description: Iranian authorities arrested dozens of people who were allegedly in a network that sold Starlink satellite terminals, as Tehran expands efforts to control information more than a month into its war with the US and Israel.

item.title: US Job Openings Fell in February, Hiring Slowed Notably
item.description: US job openings fell and hiring slowed notably in February, pointing to cooler labor demand before the war in Iran triggered additional uncertainty.

item.title: Lagarde Calls Bessent Out on Iran Damage During G-7 Video Talks
item.description: Christine Lagarde used an audience of high-level Group of Seven officials to challenge Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s optimism that the Iran war’s economic fallout will be short-lived, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Barclays Predicts Growing Treasury Market Will Need Bailouts
item.description: The Treasury market has been rendered structurally unstable by its explosive growth and is likely to require occasional “official interventions” to support its functioning, according to strategists at Barclays.

item.title: Private Equity Racks Up $94 Billion Debt in 2025 to Fund Payouts
item.description: Private equity-owned companies borrowed $94 billion in leveraged loans and high-yield bonds in the US to fund their own payouts last year, increasing risk to the businesses, according to an analysis by Moody’s Ratings.

item.title: US Junk Bonds Headed for Worst Quarterly Returns Since 2022
item.description: US high-yield bonds are on track to post their first negative quarterly returns since 2022 as fears of artificial intelligence disrupting software makers and rising Treasury yields made investors more reluctant to bet on riskier companies.

item.title: Another Greek Oil Tanker Exits Hormuz Despite Iran’s Blockade
item.description: Another Greek-controlled oil tanker has crossed the Strait of Hormuz, marking the fourth such voyage since hostilities in the Middle East began.

item.title: CLO Refinancing Plans Put On Ice in Europe as Funding Costs Rise
item.description: As the war in the Middle East drives up the cost of CLO funding, some European managers are shelving their refinancing plans — a move that may complicate efforts by the region’s riskiest borrowers to roll over their debt.

item.title: India Set for Searing Summer as Iran War Strains Energy Supplies
item.description: Most of India is forecast to experience higher than normal heat wave days through June, raising the risk of power shortages as the South Asian nation grapples with energy strains worsened by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Chile Copper Output Hits Nine-Year Low, Adding to Supply Concern
item.description: Chile posted its lowest monthly copper output in almost nine years, highlighting the challenges facing the world’s top producer as ore grades decline and key mines underperform.

item.title: Rivian Bike Spinoff Lands $1 Billion Valuation, DoorDash Partnership
item.description: Also, an electric bike and transportation startup spun out of Rivian Automotive Inc., has reached a $1 billion valuation in a new funding round, and has struck a partnership with DoorDash Inc. to work on autonomous deliveries.

item.title: Russian Oil Exports Plunge as Drone Strikes Cripple Baltic Ports
item.description: Weekly shipments from Baltic ports hit the lowest since Moscow invaded Ukraine

item.title: Axios Software Tool Used by Millions Compromised in Hack
item.description: Axios, a tool widely used to develop software applications, was compromised overnight, introducing a vulnerability in a key part of the internet’s plumbing.

item.title: US IPO Prospects in Limbo as War Dents Optimism Ahead of SpaceX
item.description: The companies that typically make up the backbone of the US initial public offering market are eyeing the Iran war for signs volatility could boil over and wreck their plans. For banks, there’s a ray of hope — that SpaceX’s potential blockbuster listing could still go ahead, and make their whole year.

item.title: Amazon Picks U.S. Bancorp as Issuer for Small-Business Cards
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. tapped U.S. Bancorp to be the issuer for two small business credit cards that the e-commerce platform is relaunching in the coming months.

item.title: Aluminum Set For Biggest Monthly Gain Since 2018 on Iran War
item.description: Aluminum headed for its biggest monthly gain in almost eight years, as the war in the Middle East disrupted supplies and damaged local production facilities, tightening the global market.

item.title: Canada Economy Grew Modestly Ahead of Iran War Energy Shock
item.description: Canada’s real gross domestic product grew modestly at the start of the year, suggesting the economy was chugging along ahead of the oil price shock fueled by the Iran war.

item.title: China’s TCL to Buy Majority Stake in Sony’s Home Entertainment
item.description: TCL Electronics Holdings Ltd. agreed to buy a majority stake in Sony Group Corp.’s global home entertainment business as the Chinese company seeks to expand overseas.

item.title: International Baccalaureate Exams Scrapped in UAE Amid Iran War
item.description: Students following the International Baccalaureate program in the United Arab Emirates will be graded on their coursework and teachers’ assessments rather than written exams this year as regional conflict disrupts the academic calendar.

item.title: Nvidia Invests $2 Billion in Marvell, Announces Partnership
item.description: Nvidia Corp. said it has invested $2 billion in Marvell Technology Inc. and that the two chipmakers plan to collaborate on silicon photonics technology.

item.title: Huawei Growth Stalls After Apple Comeback Offsets China AI Push
item.description: Huawei Technologies Co.’s growth decelerated sharply after Apple Inc.’s iPhone 17 made headway in China and domestic challengers in AI chipmaking emerged.

item.title: Tether Cuts Top Gold Traders Months After Hiring Them From HSBC
item.description: Tether Holdings SA has cut two senior precious metals traders who had joined from HSBC Holdings Plc just months ago as part of the stablecoin issuer’s plan to build “the best trading floor for gold in the world.”

item.title: Private Credit Poses ‘Chain Reaction’ Risk to US, DZ Bank Says
item.description: Germany’s second-largest lender warned that private credit is now so big, it poses a major risk to the US economy.

item.title: China Vanke Posts $12.8 Billion Loss As Bond Maturities Loom
item.description: China Vanke Co. posted a record 88.6 billion yuan ($12.8 billion) loss last year, a sign of the deepening problems facing the developer ahead of a wall of upcoming debt maturities.
